skill.name: moodle-external-api-development
skill.description: Create custom external web service APIs for Moodle LMS. Use when implementing web services for course management, user tracking, quiz operations, or custom plugin functionality. Covers parameter validation, capability checks, and service registration.
skill.summary: Create custom external web service APIs for Moodle LMS with proper security and validation patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation/educational skill for Moodle external API development. Static analyzer flagged 127 potential issues, but evaluation confirms all are false positives. The detected patterns are: (1) Markdown backticks for code formatting flagged as shell commands, (2) Documentation URLs to moodledev.io, (3) Benign keywords in YAML frontmatter triggering false positives. No actual security risks present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected backticks as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code formatting (e.g., `execute_parameters()`), not actual shell commands. No command execution risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Detected
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs found are legitimate links to Moodle developer documentation (moodledev.io) and example API endpoint placeholders. No data exfiltration risk.

content.value_statement: Create secure custom web service APIs for Moodle LMS with proper parameter validation, capability checks, and service registration patterns.
actual_capabilities: Create external API class files following Moodle's three-method pattern (execute_parameters, execute, execute_returns)
Define input parameter structures with proper validation using Moodle's external_value types
Implement business logic with context validation and capability checks
Register web services with proper permissions and AJAX/REST access
Return structured data matching defined return specifications
limitations: Does not cover Moodle admin configuration or server setup
Does not include authentication token management details
Does not provide pre-built complete plugins - only guidance

use_cases.title: Moodle Plugin Developer
use_cases.description: Build custom external APIs for your Moodle plugin to expose functionality to mobile apps or external systems

use_cases.title: LMS Integration Specialist
use_cases.description: Create web service endpoints to integrate Moodle with student information systems or CRM platforms

use_cases.title: Mobile App Backend Developer
use_cases.description: Develop Moodle-backed REST APIs for custom mobile applications accessing course data

prompt_templates.title: Basic API Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a basic external API class in Moodle with the three-method pattern

prompt_templates.title: Parameter Definition
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I define input parameters with validation for a Moodle external API? Include examples for integer, text, and boolean parameters

prompt_templates.title: Business Logic Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write example business logic for a Moodle external API that retrieves course progress, including context validation and capability checks

prompt_templates.title: Service Registration
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I register a Moodle external web service? Show the services.php file structure with capabilities and AJAX access

output_examples.input: Create an external API to get user course progress
output: PHP class extending external_api with execute_parameters(), execute(), and execute_returns() methods
Parameter definitions using external_value(PARAM_INT) and external_single_structure
Context validation using validate_context() and capability checks
Database query using parameterized queries for SQL injection prevention

output_examples.input: Register the web service for mobile access
output: services.php file with function definition including classname, methodname, and capabilities
Service definition with restrictedusers and enabled flags
Use MOODLE_OFFICIAL_MOBILE_SERVICE for mobile app integration

best_practices: Always validate parameters using validate_parameters() before processing
Use context validation and capability checks to enforce permissions
Use parameterized queries ($DB->get_records_sql) to prevent SQL injection
Document all parameter types and return structures clearly
anti_patterns: Skipping parameter validation - always use validate_parameters()
Using raw SQL without parameterized queries - use $DB methods with bound parameters
Omitting capability checks - always verify user permissions
Returning inconsistent data structures - match execute_returns() definition exactly

faq.question: What is the three-method pattern for Moodle external APIs?
faq.answer: Moodle external APIs require three methods: execute_parameters() defines input structure, execute() contains business logic, and execute_returns() defines the output structure.

faq.question: How do I secure my Moodle external API?
faq.answer: Use context validation (validate_context), capability checks (require_capability), and proper parameter validation (validate_parameters) to secure your API.

faq.question: Can I use my external API with the Moodle mobile app?
faq.answer: Yes, include MOODLE_OFFICIAL_MOBILE_SERVICE in your service registration to enable mobile access.

faq.question: What parameter types does Moodle support?
faq.answer: Moodle supports PARAM_INT, PARAM_TEXT, PARAM_RAW, PARAM_BOOL, PARAM_FLOAT, PARAM_ALPHANUMEXT, and more for external API parameters.

faq.question: How do I prevent SQL injection in external APIs?
faq.answer: Use Moodle's database API ($DB) with parameterized queries using named placeholders like :userid instead of string concatenation.

faq.question: What is the difference between read and write service types?
faq.answer: Read type is for SELECT operations that only retrieve data. Write type is for INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operations that modify data.
